doc.title: Asset Gain or Loss (Actual vs Expected Return)
doc.event: Sutter Co.'s pension plan assets were expected to earn $40,000 this year (8% expected rate on beginning plan assets of $500,000). Actual return differed from expected. Prepare the entry to defer the asset gain or loss to OCI.

lines.why: Assets grew 12,000 more than the expense calc assumed — funded status improves.
lines.trap: Expense already used the EXPECTED 40,000. Only the surprise (12,000) gets its own entry.

lines.why: The gain is deferred to OCI — it does NOT touch net income this period.
Assets underperformed by 9,000 — a loss parked in OCI, awaiting possible corridor amortization.
Funded status worsens by the shortfall.

memorize.body: Asset gain/(loss) = Actual return − Expected return
Pension expense uses EXPECTED return; the actual-vs-expected difference parks in OCI—Gain/Loss.

questions.prompt: Actual return $52,000, expected return $40,000. What asset gain is deferred to OCI?

distractors.feedback: That's the whole actual return. The gain is only the piece expense didn't already assume: actual − expected = 12,000.
That's the expected return — it already reduced pension expense. The OCI entry captures only the surprise.

build.scaffold: Direction check: did the surprise HELP or HURT funded status? Helped → debit Pension Asset/Liability.
